title: cuneiform-mcp: Four-Axis Computational Discovery in the eBL Cuneiform Corpus
description:
A four-axis computational discovery pipeline for the electronic Babylonian Library (eBL) transliteration corpus of ~36,500 cuneiform tablets. Operates on raw sign-token sequences without metadata and produces manuscript-sibling rescues, full manuscript-witness cluster reconstruction, scribal-lineage candidates, and lacuna restorations.

Headline findings: 55% sibling-rescue rate on lexical false-negatives; 100+ tablet āšipūtu (exorcist) library cluster recovered from a single seed; 3 reciprocal same-scribe pairs with cross-axis discrimination; 91.7% top-1 precision in synthetic-gap lacuna restoration.

v0.18.3 — 30 MCP tools, deterministic seeds, full reproducibility scripts. See docs/methods-paper-arxiv.md for the methods paper.
notes: Concurrent submission to Cuneiform Digital Library Journal (methods paper). Corresponds to commit 4976266 (v0.18.3).
